title: The Door of the Sheepfold
subtitle: Christ teaches that no one enters the fold of the sheep except through the lowly door — through Him

stillness: Be still, wretched sinner, and grow small.
The door into the fold is low; only those who bend may pass.
He enters by the door who enters through Christ — who knows the humility of Christ.
gospel: Amen, amen, I say to you:
He who enters not by the door into the sheepfold,
that man is a thief and a robber.
But he who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
and the sheep hear his voice;
and he calls his own sheep by name,
And the sheep follow him, for they know his voice;
but a stranger they will not follow,
but flee from him, for they know not the voice of strangers.
behold: Attend to each thing as though you stood there present.
Behold the fold of the sheep: it is the catholic Church, under one shepherd, Christ the Lord.
Behold the door, low and narrow — Christ Himself, the door of truth, of liberty, of free goodness, of simplicity.
See the proud bruise themselves against the lowness of it, and the greedy unable to squeeze through the narrowness.
See the thief who tunnels under the wall in the dark, stealing the sheep in secret, the Lord unwilling.
See the robber who breaks in by open violence, to thrust himself in by force.
But the true shepherd enters by the door, and the porter opens to him.
Behold him call his own sheep by name, coming down to each one in familiar tenderness, that each may dare to come to him.
He leads them out of the darkness of error into the light of truth,
out of the sorrow of bondage into the kingdom of liberty,
from the prison of guilt into the freedom of grace.
He goes before them by the example of good works and of life,
and the sheep follow his footsteps, for they know his voice and delight in it.
consider: Consider, then, by which door you have come.
There is no entering except through the lowly door, and you cannot pass through it unless you humble yourself and think no high things of yourself.
Neither wisdom, nor the keeping of the Law, nor a good life is worth anything — except through Him.
It is impossible to see without Christ, impossible to reach the truth without Him.
He who climbs up another way is a thief and a robber: such are all the unfaithful, and even the evil faithful.
The ambitious climb up by ladders from above, with Lucifer, with Dathan and Abiram, whom the earth swallowed alive for their ambition.
The greedy break through the wall, with Simon Magus.
The heretics dig away the foundations, with Arius.
But mark this: there are three gifts men snatch at when they hasten into office —
the gift from the heart, which is favor and grace;
the gift from the mouth, which is the flattery of the crowd;
the gift from the hand, which is the giving of reward.
Whoever shakes himself free of these, and enters without seeking them, blessed shall he be.
And to the one who enters otherwise it shall be said: Friend, how camest thou in here, not having a wedding garment?
And he shall be cast out from among the sheep, into the exterior darkness.
prayer: who for Thy sheep didst lay down Thy life as their ransom,
Thy flesh for their food, and Thy blood for their drink;
who wast made for us the door into the Church militant and triumphant,
that through Thee we might enter, to be saved, in unto Thee:
and look upon us in mercy, directing us into the way of salvation,
that we may know Thee, and be conformed to Thee by imitating Thee.
Let us not hear the voice of strangers — of the world, the flesh, and the devil —
but Thine alone, obeying Thy commandments and Thy counsels,
that we may have the life of grace, and more abundantly the life of glory,
and may find with Thee the pastures of eternal refreshment.

latinJewel.latin: Intrat per ostium, qui intrat per Christum, qui imitatur passionem Christi, qui cognoscit humilitatem Christi.
latinJewel.english: He enters by the door who enters through Christ — he who imitates the Passion of Christ, who knows the humility of Christ.
